lever_balance_diagram: The image shows a horizontal lever beam with a fulcrum, numbered distance marks, and weight blocks.

This diagram shows a lever beam with numbered distance marks and weight blocks on the beam.
The figure shows a lever setup with a fulcrum, distance marks, and weight blocks.

side_torque_value: What is the total torque from the weights on the {torque_side} side of the fulcrum?
Using the shown {torque_side}-side weights and their distances from the fulcrum, what torque is produced?
Determine the torque contributed by the weights on the {torque_side} side of the lever.
What total {torque_side}-side torque is shown by the lever diagram?
Read the {torque_side}-side weights and distance marks. What torque do they produce about the fulcrum?
missing_weight_balance_value: What weight should replace the marked `?` block so the lever balances?
A missing weight is marked with `?`. What value would balance the lever?
How large should the marked `?` weight be so the torques on both sides match?
Use the shown weights and distances. What value must the marked `?` weight have for the lever to balance?
What integer weight is required in the marked `?` block so the beam is balanced about the fulcrum?

task:side_torque_value_query.annotation_hint: set "annotation" to an array of [x0,y0,x1,y1] pixel boxes around every weight block on the queried side of the fulcrum
task:side_torque_value_query.answer_hint: set "answer" to the integer torque value

task:missing_weight_balance_value_query.annotation_hint: set "annotation" to an object with keys "known_weights" and "target_weight"; each value is an array of [x0,y0,x1,y1] pixel boxes around the known weight blocks or the marked `?` weight block used for the balance
task:missing_weight_balance_value_query.answer_hint: set "answer" to the integer missing weight value
